audit_items.proof_trace: set_allocation_lifetime_ev is a void function — it has no return value, making the property's assertion ('r != NULL before deref(r)') vacuously inapplicable. The function merely stores the ev timer handle into lifetime_ev. All downstream accesses to lifetime_ev are through the IOA_EVENT_DEL macro (ns_turn_ioalib.h:211), which guards with `if(E)` before dereferencing, and delete_ioa_timer also NULL-guards at entry (ns_ioalib_engine_impl.c:611), so even a NULL ev (e.g. from malloc failure in set_ioa_timer) cannot cause a null pointer dereference.
